breaking.title: Morning Digest: U.S. launches more strikes on Iran; 27 killed in Bangkok pub fire, and more
breaking.summary: Here is a select list of stories to start the day

breaking.title: Earthquake of magnitude 3.6 hits J&K’s Baramulla
breaking.summary: There were no reports of any damage or casualties

breaking.title: Oil jumps 3% after US, Iran escalate strikes in Mideast
breaking.summary: Oil prices climbed Monday as Iran expanded strikes on Gulf states.  U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude rose and Brent crude futures climbed.  Tehran extended strikes on Qatar and the United Arab Emirates over the weekend.  This cycle of attacks casts doubt on an interim U.S.-Iranian agreement.  Market analysts suggest the current flare-up represents an escalation within a fragile truce.

breaking.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
breaking.summary: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

breaking.title: West Asia war updates: Projectiles hit Iran's Gulf island of Qeshm, state media says
breaking.summary: U.S. attacks Iran over ship being hit in Hormuz; Tehran responds by hitting Arab states

breaking.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
breaking.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

breaking.title: Global Market Today: Asian shares slip as oil jumps on Gulf attacks
breaking.summary: Asian markets declined as intensified Gulf fighting and oil price surges rekindled inflation risks.  Oil prices climbed significantly, impacting global economic outlooks and investor sentiment.  Investors increased expectations for a Federal Reserve interest rate hike soon.  Major banks and tech companies are set to release their quarterly earnings reports.  South Korean chip stocks faced pressure, impacting the broader semiconductor sector globally.

breaking.title: Police say no suggestion of political motive in Widdecombe killing after new arrest
breaking.summary: Police say they are not looking for anyone else after a 28-year-old man was arrested in Rotherham, South Yorkshire.
